meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Debugging Woes
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit with Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your development and build professional apps.
content.answer_block: Debugging is the primary hurdle for student developers. By adopting a pre-configured SaaS starter kit, you inherit tested architecture and robust error-handling patterns. Coupling this with AI-driven agents like Antigravity and Claude Code allows you to automate root-cause analysis, turning hours of frustration into actionable, production-ready code fixes immediately.
content.intro:
You’ve been staring at the monitor for three hours. The error logs are a cascading waterfall of cryptic stack traces, and your 'simple' feature implementation has spiraled into a dependency-hell nightmare. It feels like you're fighting the language itself, and the dream of launching your first SaaS feels impossibly distant.

But here is the industry secret: elite engineers don't debug by guessing; they build on foundations that don't break. By leveraging a high-performance SaaS starter kit, you bypass the boilerplate fatigue and move straight to shipping. It’s time to stop manually debugging every line and start orchestrating AI tools to do the heavy lifting for you.

steps.title: Bootstrap with a Proven SaaS Starter Kit
steps.description: Select a production-ready starter kit that includes authentication, database ORMs, and error logging. This ensures your foundation is stable before you write a single line of custom logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project structure and recommend a modular architecture that separates business logic from data access layers to minimize future debugging surface area.

steps.title: Contextualize Error Logs with Claude Code
steps.description: Instead of copy-pasting errors blindly, use Claude Code to parse the specific file context and the stack trace simultaneously to identify the exact line of failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain the logic flow leading to this specific stack trace: [INSERT STACK TRACE]. Identify if the issue is a race condition or a type mismatch.

steps.title: Refactor Logic using Antigravity Patterns
steps.description: Use Antigravity to refactor messy, bug-prone code blocks into cleaner, testable functions that follow SOLID principles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this function to be idempotent and include comprehensive input validation to prevent runtime crashes.

steps.title: Automate Integration Testing
steps.description: Generate integration tests that simulate real user behavior to catch bugs before they reach your deployment environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of integration tests for the authentication flow that covers edge cases like expired tokens and invalid user roles.

steps.title: Continuous Monitoring and AI Patching
steps.description: Establish a feedback loop where production logs are automatically piped into your AI workflow to suggest fixes before users report bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a monitoring script that alerts me when the error rate exceeds 1% and suggests a code fix based on the last 5 logs.

pro_tips: Always wrap external API calls in a 'Circuit Breaker' pattern; it prevents cascading failures when third-party services lag.
Use 'Type-Driven Development'—define your data interfaces before writing logic to eliminate 80% of runtime errors.
Never debug in production; maintain an identical local staging environment using Docker containers.
Keep your git commits atomic; if a feature breaks, you should be able to identify the exact commit that introduced the regression.
Utilize 'Logging Decorators' to automatically capture function arguments and return values without polluting your core business logic.
content.next_steps: After completing this workflow, deploy your application to a cloud provider like Vercel or Railway and set up automated error monitoring with Sentry to keep your production environment healthy.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder with a professional workflow. By mastering these tools, you've cut your debugging time by 70% and gained a massive competitive edge in the job market. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
